twitter_thread: Regenerative Adult Stem Cells matters more than people think. Here's why → 🧵
1/ The default approach to Regenerative Adult Stem Cells wastes time and money.
2/ The cause: most resources oversimplify or assume too much.
3/ The fix is three moves: survey, default, iterate.
4/ Survey: find the 3 leading options, ignore long-tail.
5/ Default: pick one that covers 80% of cases.
6/ Iterate: time-box a trial, measure honestly.
7/ That's it. Save this thread for later.
pack.linkedin:
Regenerative Adult Stem Cells is one of those topics where most takes are either too generic or too in-the-weeds.

Here is a framework that has worked in practice: start with a survey, pick a default, iterate on a trial window. The point is to make the first decision quickly, then learn from doing.

If you are evaluating Regenerative Adult Stem Cells right now and feeling stuck, the cheapest mistake is overthinking. Pick the option that covers the most cases, set a date when you will review it, and start.
